findings.detail: safeHref allows any URL whose first character is `/`. Strings like `//evil.com/path` (protocol-relative) therefore pass through unchanged into href/src from inlineFormat link and image handlers. Attacker-controlled markdown in uploaded .md inputs, generated reports, or report history can inject `` / ``, enabling phishing and cookie-scoped attacks on the current host scheme.
findings.fix: Use a strict allowlist (e.g. only http: and https: with URL parsing), reject protocol-relative URLs explicitly, or normalize with `new URL(url, location.origin)` and verify `protocol` is `http:` or `https:`.

findings.detail: Input file text from `/api/.../inputs/...` is rendered as HTML via simpleMarkdown. Together with permissive safeHref and intentional ``, ``, ``, etc., this is a stored XSS/phishing vector for anyone who can upload or replace project inputs (e.g. malicious .md).
findings.fix: Render markdown in an isolated iframe/sandbox, use a vetted sanitizer (DOMPurify) on the output, or show markdown as plain text / use textContent-only preview.

findings.detail: Report body from API is inserted as HTML. If report content is influenced by untrusted data (LLM, CI, compromised backend), same markdown+safeHref issues apply. Html format branch uses textContent (safe); md branch uses innerHTML.
findings.fix: Sanitize simpleMarkdown output; tighten URL policy; or render markdown server-side to a sanitized fragment.

findings.detail: Historical report content loaded by id is rendered like loadReport() non-html path. Persisted reports could carry malicious markdown if an attacker can influence stored report text.
findings.fix: Same as line 2191; validate report provenance and sanitize output.

findings.detail: stage.stage from API overview is concatenated into a double-quoted HTML attribute without esc(). A malicious or buggy backend could send a stage key containing `"` to break out of data-tab and inject attributes or close the tag.
findings.fix: Use esc() on the attribute value, or build steps with DOM APIs (element.dataset.tab = ...) and textContent for labels.

findings.detail: nextTab is derived from tabMap[pipeline[j].stage] || pipeline[j].stage without escaping. Same attribute-injection class as the pipeline stepper loop.
findings.fix: Wrap nextTab with esc() or set data-tab via dataset after creating the element.

findings.detail: s.lines is interpolated without esc(). If the API ever returns a string (e.g. `"
findings.fix: Use esc(String(s.lines != null ? s.lines : "-")) or coerce to number and reject invalid values.

findings.detail: Post-hoc regex stripping of script tags and on* attributes is fragile (parser differential, SVG/math, encoding tricks). Primary risk is still intentional markdown HTML plus weak URL policy, not script-tag bypass.
findings.fix: Prefer allowlist-based sanitization after markdown parse, or do not emit raw HTML from markdown.

findings.code_snippet: return '' + text + '';
findings.detail: Link label `text` is inserted without esc(), but in simpleMarkdown all call sites pass `text` through esc(line) or esc(cell) before inlineFormat, so angle brackets should be entity-encoded. Risk is mainly maintenance: future callers of inlineFormat on raw strings would be vulnerable.
findings.fix: Escape `text` in the link replacer (esc(text)) for defense in depth.

findings.detail: Generic HTML sink. Current builders (detailField, show*Detail) escape user strings with esc() and inject only known-safe HTML fragments (badges, lists, esc'd pre). Second argument to detailField is often intentional HTML: priorityBadge/statusBadge (esc inside), detailTagList (esc per item), stepsHtml (esc per step), formatBytes/formatDuration (numeric), mapped-script buttons (esc on names/ids).
findings.fix: Keep using DOM APIs for dynamic panels or centralize on a single safe HTML builder; continue ensuring every user field uses esc().

findings.code_snippet: <input type="checkbox" class="case-checkbox" data-case-id="' + esc(c.id) + '" onclick="event.stopPropagation(); updateBulkActions();">
findings.detail: Inline onclick is static (not user-controlled). data-case-id uses esc(c.id). CSP may still flag inline handlers if enforced.
findings.fix: Replace with addEventListener on tbody delegation (pattern already used elsewhere) and drop onclick attribute.

findings.detail: Not innerHTML, but testData.status from API is concatenated into className without validation; unusual values could add unexpected classes or spaces (minor UI/confusion, not typical HTML injection).
findings.fix: Allowlist status values or use element.classList.toggle with known tokens only.

notes.detailField_value_html_callers:
Intentional HTML (not plain esc'd text): priorityBadge(), statusBadge(), detailTagList(), ''+esc(type)+'', steps
    /
  1. built with esc per field, formatBytes(script.size), String(script.lines), formatDuration, returncodeVal (badge HTML with esc(rc)), mapped script/case drill buttons (esc on attributes and text), '
    '+esc(output)+'
    '.
notes.read_only_innerHTML: Line 53 reads innerHTML for button restore; line 78 reads innerHTML inside esc() implementation (encoding helper).
